Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Defendants Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Their Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-02-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Samantha Ortiz, Esq. (213.480.6200; sortiz@younessilaw.com) of Law Offices of Ramin R. Younessi for Plaintiff (audio only).
Ronnie Arenas, Esq. (310.712.6171; rarenas@kelleydrye.com) of Kelley Drye & Warren LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS WALSH CONSTRUCTION COMPANY II, LLC and WALSH CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied.

The grounds for a motion for judgment on the pleadings "shall appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter of which the court is required to take judicial notice [or] a matter of which the court may take judicial notice pursuant to Section 452 or 453 of the Evidence Code." (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. sec. 438(d).)

Defendants' motion is based entirely upon a collective bargaining agreement ("CBA") which allegedly governs Plaintiffs' employment. However, the CBA is nowhere mentioned in or attached to Plaintiffs' complaint. The Court also declines to take judicial notice of the CBA. Defendants fail to authenticate the CBA and the question of whether Plaintiffs are governed by the CBA it is reasonably subject to dispute. (See Cal. Evid. Code sec. 452(h); Evid. Code sec. 1400 et seq.; Jolley v. Chase Home Fin., LLC (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 872, 889 ["we hold that judicial notice was not properly taken of the content of the P & A Agreement even if there was no dispute about its authenticity. A fortiori here, where the very authenticity of the Agreement was in dispute."].) Accordingly, no grounds exist for Defendants' motion for judgment on the pleadings.

Order is signed.
